definition: Represent each molecule with a circular Morgan fingerprint, compute Tanimoto similarity between each evaluation molecule and all training molecules, and use either the full train-eval pairwise graph or the maximum train-neighbor similarity as the SPECTRA overlap axis.
example_usage: python molecule_morgan_tanimoto.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --train-id-col train_id --eval-id-col sample_id --smiles-col smiles

citations.title: UMAP-based clustering split for rigorous evaluation of AI models for virtual screening on cancer cell lines

evidence.summary: The paper states that chemical similarity is assessed with Tanimoto coefficients on Morgan fingerprints.
The paper computes each test molecule's maximum Tanimoto similarity to training molecules using radius-2, 2048-bit Morgan fingerprints.

citations.title: BOOM: Benchmarking Out-Of-distribution Molecular Property Predictions of Machine Learning Models

evidence.summary: BOOM motivates systematic molecular OOD prediction audits and reports substantially higher OOD error than in-distribution error.

limitations: Captures 2D structural resemblance, not assay, protein target, experimental protocol, or property-range shift.
Scaffold novelty can remain incomplete because different scaffolds may still have high fingerprint similarity.
Fingerprint radius and bit length affect measured overlap.
quality_gates: Report fingerprint radius and bit length.
Plot the distribution of max train similarity by split or spectral bin.
Check whether high similarity persists across nominal scaffold or cluster splits.
